title: Masala Japanese Bread Flatbread
description: A spicy, flaky flatbread with instant yeast and a hint of Japanese citrus, perfect for a cozy snack.
instructions: In a bowl, combine flour, salt, sugar, instant yeast, garam masala, chili flakes, garlic powder, and coconut oil; stir until dry ingredients are mixed.
Add water and lemon zest; mix until a sticky dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for 5 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place dough in a greased bowl, cover, and let rise in a warm spot for 30 minutes until doubled.
Punch down the dough, divide into 4 equal portions, roll each into a flat circle about 1/4 inch thick.
Preheat the oven to 220°C; arrange the flatbreads on a parchment-lined baking sheet.
Brush the tops with a mixture of coconut oil and chopped cilantro; sprinkle extra chili flakes if desired.
Bake for 8 minutes, then broil for 1 minute until edges are golden.
Remove from oven and let cool for 5 minutes; drizzle with lemon juice before serving.
Serve warm as a spicy snack or side.
